chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَمُرُّ عَلَى طَرِيقٍ مِنْ طُرُقِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَتُصِيبُ دَابَّتُهُ إِنْسَاناً بِرِجْلِهَا فَقَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِرِجْلِهَا وَ لَكِنْ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِيَدِهَا لِأَنَّ رِجْلَهَا خَلْفَهُ إِنْ رَكِبَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَائِدَهَا فَإِنَّهُ يَمْلِكُ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ يَدَهَا يَضَعُهَا حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ

قَالَ وَ سُئِلَ عَنْ بُخْتِيٍّ اغْتَلَمَ فَخَرَجَ مِنَ الدَّارِ فَقَتَلَ رَجُلًا فَجَاءَ أَخُو الرَّجُلِ فَضَرَبَ الْفَحْلَ بِالسَّيْفِ فَعَقَرَهُ فَقَالَ صَاحِبُ الْبُخْتِيِّ ضَامِنٌ لِلدِّيَةِ وَ يَقْبِضُ ثَمَنَ بُخْتِيِّهِ
وَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُنَفِّرُ بِالرَّجُلِ فَيَعْقِرُهُ وَ تَعْقِرُ دَابَّتُهُ رَجُلًا آخَرَ فَقَالَ هُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا كَانَ مِنْ شَيْ‏ءٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having been asked about the man who passes upon a road from the roads of the Muslim, so his animal hits a human being by its (hind) legs. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon him what it hit by its (hind) legs, but upon him is what it hits by its hands (front legs), because its (hind) legs are behind him if he is riding, and if he was guiding it, so he was in control of its hands (front legs), by the Permission of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, to place these wherever he so wishes to’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘And he<sup>asws</sup> was asked about a camel in heat, so it went out from the house and killed a man. So a brother of the man came and struck the stallion with the sword and he slew it. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The owner of the camel is responsible of the wergild and would seize the price of his camel’.
And about the man who caused the man to flee, so another man slew him and his animal. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He is responsible for whatever was from something’.
